parameters.description:
MsgSubmitResult defines the MsgSubmitResult message.

The submitted solution is the theoretical probability distribution (case A)
the participant computed for the random circuit identified by round_id.

TODO: A future `random_circuit_sampling` module will define a separate
problem whose solution carries empirical shot counts (case B). This module
stays focused on the analytical/theoretical distribution.
parameters.name: body

quantumchain.random_circuit.v1.Distribut.description:
Distribution carries the theoretical (case A) probability distribution
produced by a participant solving a random-circuit problem.

`probabilities` maps each computational-basis state (e.g. "00101") to its
probability encoded as a fixed-point decimal string. String encoding keeps
the canonical proto representation deterministic across SDKs and avoids
floating-point round-off in consensus.

TODO: When the `random_circuit_sampling` module is introduced for case B,
it will use its own payload type (e.g. ShotCounts) rather than reusing this
Distribution message.

quantumchain.random_circuit.v1.MsgSubmit.description:
MsgSubmitResult defines the MsgSubmitResult message.

The submitted solution is the theoretical probability distribution (case A)
the participant computed for the random circuit identified by round_id.

TODO: A future `random_circuit_sampling` module will define a separate
problem whose solution carries empirical shot counts (case B). This module
stays focused on the analytical/theoretical distribution.
